groups.context_description: Term names faithfulness as a stable inner disposition of reliability and integrity — whether as a divine attribute, a Spirit-produced fruit, or a moral requirement of the covenant community
Term names the inner condition of unbelief — the inner non-reception of divine truth, linked to spiritual blindness, defiled mind, and defiled conscience
Term names the unbelieving as a moral category of persons — those whose inner orientation is faithlessness, contrasted with the believing community
Term names the act of inner disbelief — the refusal or failure to receive testimony or truth as credible; the inner non-assent to what is presented
Term names inner faithlessness — the failure of inner reliability and covenant fidelity, contrasted with divine faithfulness
Term names the inner state of firm conviction — being fully persuaded and settled in what has been learned and believed
Term names treachery as an inner moral condition — the act and disposition of betrayal as a defining moral characteristic
Term names the treacherous as those whose outward conduct conceals an inward failure of wholehearted devotion — treachery as the gap between inner state and outward profession
Term names treachery as an inner moral character — the disposition of faithlessness and betrayal as a defining quality of the person
Term names faith as the inner act and disposition of trust — the inner orientation of reliance on God or Christ that receives righteousness, access, and life
Term names faith as a corporate body of belief and the community that holds it — the faith as the content of Christian confession and the gathering of those who share it
Term names unfaithfulness as breach of covenant faith with God — the inner act of betrayal that violates the covenant relationship with the Lord
Term names the inner state of faithlessness that produces shame — faithlessness as the inner moral condition with consequences of exile, desolation, and communal grief
Term names unfaithfulness in intimate covenant relationship — breaking faith with a spouse or neighbour as an inner moral act

Term names the act of breaking faith — the inner volitional act of covenant unfaithfulness that ruptures relationship with the Lord, whether in sacral violation, national apostasy, or intimate covenant
Term names divine faithfulness as the reliable inner character of God — his steadfast love and faithfulness as the ground of covenant, prayer, and praise
Term names human faithfulness as the inner quality of integrity and reliability in covenant — walking before God with a whole heart in truthfulness
Term names the absence of faithfulness as moral crisis — truth perished, faithfulness gone; the inner condition of a community that has abandoned integrity
Term names the inner condition of diminished faith — the insufficient inner trust that gives rise to fear, doubt, and anxious self-reliance
Term names the inner condition of insufficient faith — a diminished or weak inner trust that falls short of what is needed or called for
Term names the inner act of believing and trusting — the fundamental inner disposition of reception and reliance on God, Christ, or his word, producing life and righteousness
Term names believing that reveals the limits or conditions of faith — sign-dependent believing, intellectual assent without transformation, or the demons' shuddering belief
Term names believers as the community constituted by the inner act of having believed — defined by belief as the constitutive past event
Term names treachery as faithlessness toward God — the inner act of covenant betrayal directed against the Lord
Term names the treacherous as a moral category of persons — those whose inner character is marked by betrayal; whose way leads to their ruin
Term names treachery in personal relationship — the inner act of betrayal directed against a brother, friend, or companion
Term names faithfulness as the inner moral quality of reliability and integrity — the character of being trustworthy in word, witness, and covenant relationship
Term names the faithful as a moral category of persons — those whose inner character of reliability marks them as belonging to a community of trust before God
Term names divine faithfulness as an enduring inner attribute — God's reliability extending across all generations, undergirding his covenant
Term names human faithfulness as an inner moral quality chosen and practised — reliability and integrity in conduct before God and community
Term names faithfulness as the inner quality whose absence constitutes moral and spiritual crisis — truth and faithfulness perished in the land
Term names divine faithfulness as the reliable inner character of God — he cannot deny himself; he will do what he has promised
Term names human faithfulness as the inner quality demonstrated in reliable service — the faithful person is reliable over little and over much, in the Lord and before people
Term names believers as a moral category — the faithful as those who trust in Christ, contrasted with the unbelieving
Term names the obtaining of eschatological realities — salvation, resurrection, a better life — as the object of inner hope and endurance
Term names the Spirit's intercession within the person — the divine inner advocacy that arises from human weakness and expresses itself in groanings beyond articulation
Term names the inner act of intercession — entreaty or pleading on behalf of another before God, or the absence of such intercession as a moral absence
Term names the substitutionary laying of iniquity on another — the moral burden of the many placed on the one who bears it
Term names the act of intercession — the inner act of pleading before God on behalf of another, or the divine advocacy undertaken on behalf of the person
Term names praise as the defining inner orientation of the person before God — the mouth and heart continually directed toward declaring God's worth
Term names God himself as the content and source of praise — what the inner life is oriented toward and what enables praise to rise
Term names praise as a transforming garment — replacing the faint spirit with the inner orientation of worship
Term names the shining of divine light upon the person — God's illuminating presence as the condition of the inner life's flourishing and orientation
Term names thanksgiving to God as a whole-person inner act — the deliberate orientation of the entire inner being toward gratitude and acknowledgement of God's character and works
Term names confession of sin as an inner-being act of transparency before God — the acknowledgment of guilt that enables forgiveness and restoration
Term names the acknowledgement of God's name as the inner-being act of returning to God — the turning of the heart toward God as condition of answered prayer and restoration
Term names the inner-sourced praise that comes from God — the true commendation that reaches the hidden life of the heart, not external human approval
Term names the doxological purpose orientation — all of life directed toward the praise of God's glory
Term names the inner orientation of the mind toward what is praiseworthy — thinking on praiseworthy things as a discipline of the inner life
Term names the act of singing praise as a whole-person inner orientation — heart, soul, spirit, and voice directed toward God in musical worship
Term names praise as the universal inner-being response to God — the call to all peoples to orient themselves toward God in vocal exaltation
Term names song as the inner-being expression of orientation toward God — the statutes, the praises, and the voice from the night all flowing from the inner life
Term names the inner-being act of praise directed toward God — gratitude for wisdom given, or awe at divine sovereignty
Term names praise misdirected to false gods — the inner orientation of worship toward objects that cannot see, hear, or know
Term names praise as the spontaneous inner-being response to God's action — whether from children or crowds, the outward expression of inward recognition and awe
Term names singing praise as an integrated inner-being act — engaging spirit, mind, and heart simultaneously in musical worship toward God
Term names the inner-being act of beautifying God through praise — the person's deliberate orientation toward adorning God with worship
Term names praise-celebration as an inner-being act of devotion — whether rightly directed toward God or misdirected toward false gods
Term names the thanksgiving sacrifice as an inner-being act of worship — the offering that glorifies God and expresses the grateful orientation of the whole person
Term names thanksgiving as song and communal expression — the inner-being of gratitude voiced in congregation, procession, and restored community
Term frames corporate confession of sin and moral failure — the we of communal acknowledgment of guilt and wrong before God
Term frames corporate commitment or lament — the we of communal inner will directed toward God or communal distress expressed before him
Term names the oneness of covenantal union — the 'one flesh' or 'one heart and one way' that defines the depth of relationship between persons, or between God and his people
Term names the corporate oneness of inner resolve — the united will of a community described as one man or one people
Term names the uniqueness of God or of the irreplaceable — the one that shapes the weight of divine identity or the cost of a singular relationship
Term names the quantifier that intensifies inner-being experience — the one that shapes the subjective inner weight
Term names the beginning that discloses an inner-being condition — either the moment when prayer begins and God responds immediately, or the role of leadership in praise
Term names the inner-being quality of wisdom-restraint — the capacity to soothe and hold back what the spirit would otherwise vent
Term names the high/exalted praise that rises from the inner life — the extolling of God as the fullest expression of the inner-being's worship capacity
Term names praise as a test of inner character — what a person does with praise reveals their inner moral formation
Term names the glory of God as the supreme inner-being orientation of the human person — what is sought, hoped for, and the measure against which all human fallenness is assessed
Term names glory as transforming — the process by which the inner person is changed from one degree of glory to another through beholding the Lord
Term names doxological inner orientation — the purpose of all human action and the direction of the inner life toward the glorification of God
Term names eschatological glory — the future condition of the person and creation fully realized in glory, which functions as inner hope sustaining the present
Term names the inner-being act of confessing/acknowledging — the orientation of the person or of Christ toward public declaration of God's worth or a person's identity
Term names the singing of praise as an inner-being act of worship — declaring God's name in congregational song
Term names praise as a sacrificial inner-being act — the offering of the inner life to God expressed through deliberate vocal acknowledgement
Term names the sustained inner orientation of praise toward God — the deliberate, personal direction of the whole being in worship
Term names corporate/liturgical praise — the community's inner-being act of worship organized for God's service
Term names the praise of the worthy — what and who deserves to be praised, as a reflection of inner-being discernment and moral orientation
Term names the boast of the inner life directed toward God — glorying in God as the proper inner orientation of the redeemed
Term names the wrongful boast — the inner orientation of pride directed toward self, wealth, or wickedness
Term names the shining figure whose pride produces catastrophic inner-spiritual collapse — the inner-being of pride that leads to being cast down
Term names the inner-being act of thanks and praise — the deliberate, sustained orientation of the person toward God in gratitude, whether in revelation or under persecution
Term names the call to extol God — the inner-being orientation of all peoples toward God in praise
Term names the psalm/song as a designated form of inner-being expression — the compositional act of directing one's whole inner life toward God in structured musical-poetic form
Term names music as the instrument of coerced worship — the signal that tests whether the inner being will orient toward the true God or capitulate to false worship under threat
Term names melody as the vehicle of inner-being worship — both its proper expression and the failure of melody when detached from inner integrity
Term names the Lord himself as the inner song of the redeemed — the person's strength and salvation expressed as song, God being the content of that song
Term names prayer as the sustained inner orientation of the person toward God — the discipline, posture, and practice of bringing the whole self before God
Term names prayer as the inner-being medium of access and petition — through which faith receives, the saints's needs ascend, and God responds
Term names petition as the inner-being act of bringing specific need before God — the orientation of the whole person toward God in earnest, sometimes anguished, request
Term names the whispered prayer — the desperate, intimate inner-being cry poured out when under divine discipline
Term names intercession as an inner-being act — the petition offered on behalf of others or for sanctification of that which is received
Term names vow and prayer as inner-being acts of commitment and faith — the binding of the will to God and the trusting petition that acts on that commitment
Term names earnest petition to God — the inner-being act of urgent, sometimes desperate, prayer directed toward God or Jesus
Term names apostolic entreaty — the earnest inner-being appeal from one person to another calling them toward God, reconciliation, or transformation
Term names prayer as the inner-being act of bringing the will before God — including surrender, petition, and watchfulness; modelled supremely in Jesus's Gethsemane prayers
Term names prayer as the instructed inner-being discipline — the shape, posture, and conditions of authentic prayer taught by Jesus
Term names corporate and intercessory prayer — the community's inner-being act of praying together, or one person's inner orientation toward another in prayer
Term names the seeking of God as the primary inner orientation of the person — the whole-hearted turning toward God in worship, obedience, and relational pursuit
Term names the act of seeking divine guidance through inquiry of the Lord — expressing inner dependence and orientation toward God's will, often in contrast to seeking false gods
Term names seeking evil or worthless things as an inner orientation away from God — pursuing harm, seeking the favour of idols, or seeking what profits nothing
Term names God seeking his people — the divine inner-relational initiative of pursuing, caring for, and requiring accountability from his flock
Term names the finding of relational favour or grace — finding favour in another's eyes as an inner relational state of approval, goodwill, and acceptance
Term names the finding of wisdom, understanding, or inner character qualities — the discovery of what is truly good through inner-directed searching
Term names God finding sin, iniquity, or faithfulness in a person — the divine discernment of the inner moral condition of a person or community
Term names finding rest, refuge, comfort, or the absence of such — the inner state of having found what one needs, or the distress of finding no help or relief

Term emphasises the personal inner-relational commitment of God or a human speaker — the emphatic "I" foregrounding inner states of fear, love, faithfulness, covenant commitment, or relational orientation
Term names earnest inner-directed seeking — the soul and spirit yearning and seeking God, particularly in distress or need, expressing deep inner orientation toward divine encounter
Term names the inner decision to attempt an action — whether motivated by hostile intent or by presumptuous religious initiative

Term introduces the inner source of action, identity, or righteousness — from the heart, from faith, from God, from grace — disclosing the inner-being origin of obedience, spiritual life, and right standing
Term introduces source in the context of resurrection and new life — out of death, from among the dead — signalling the inner transformation of the person through divine power

Inner persuasion, trust, and conviction — the state of being inwardly convinced, of trusting or relying upon someone or something, and of having confidence in the Lord; including the warnings against confidence in the flesh and the reassurance of the heart
Trust as inner disposition of reliance on God — source of strength
Inner disposition of trust/security as relational posture toward God
Security as covenantal/eschatological promise — divinely granted dwelling in safety
Personal security as inner fruit of integrity and wise living
Confident assurance directed toward God or Christ — inner disposition of relational trust upward
Confidence as inner disposition of assurance toward persons or in one's own standing
Inner reliance on God as the proper object of trust
Misplaced or hypocritical inner reliance on wrong objects — people, military, self
Divine support as inner-being ground of confidence in calamity
Divine support as inner-being ground of confidence in calamity
Trust in God expressed through courageous inner commitment at cost to self
Term names worship as the act of prostration and bowing before God — the complete physical and inner-being orientation of the person in submission and reverence
Term names idolatrous prostration — bowing to other gods or images as the inner-being act of misplaced worship
Term names social prostration as an act of relational deference — the physical expression of honour, petition, or submission before persons of higher standing
Term names genuine worship as the inner-being act of prostration and devotion directed toward God or Christ — the complete orientation of the person in reverent submission
Term names idolatrous worship as the inner-being act of prostration misdirected toward false gods, demons, or the beast — the same devotional capacity wrongly oriented
Term names social prostration as an act of relational deference — a physical expression of respect ranging from courtesy to conditional surrender
Term names worship-prostration as an inner devotional response to divine revelation — recognition of God's presence and truth
Term names commanded or coerced prostration before a human image — the false worship system that demands inner-being allegiance, which the faithful refuse
Term names worship/service as the inner devotional orientation of the person directed toward God — enacted in prayer, fasting, and faithful witness
Term names service rendered to idols or false gods — the inner devotion misplaced toward counterfeit objects
Term names worship as a priestly/liturgical act — ordered service in the sanctuary expressing the community's inner orientation toward God
Term names service of God as the directed inner orientation of the person — the wholehearted devotion that God requires and defines covenantal faithfulness
Term names service of idols and false gods as the inner-being act of devotion misdirected — Israel's persistent defection from the Lord
Term names worship as a divinely oriented inner service — the person's orientation of devoted service toward God, enacted in cultic and spiritual forms
Term names priestly or Levitical ministry as a devoted inner vocation — standing before God to serve, a calling requiring committed inner orientation
Term names personal attendance and service as an expression of relational devotion — the servant who attends and supports those they serve
Term names worship as a directed inner orientation toward a divine object — naming the inner posture of piety
Term qualifies worship as spiritual or rational — arising from the inner person rather than being merely external ritual
Term names religion or worship as an organised inner orientation toward a divine object — designating the person's devotional disposition
Term names ministry as a sacred service rendered to God and to the community — an act of devotion expressing inner commitment
Term names the pit of destruction as existential peril — the state of being trapped or facing annihilation, from which God delivers
Term names the pit as a metaphor for moral self-entrapment — the consequence of misleading others returns upon the one who set it
Term designates a moral character type whose inner disposition is hostile to the holy — those who by nature or choice oppose what is sacred
Term names the true worshiper as a person defined by their inner-being orientation — worship in spirit and truth as the constitutive identity of those whom God seeks
Term names prostration in worship misdirected toward idols — the physical expression of inner devotion wrongly oriented
Term names deliberate inner intention or scheming — the inner act of purposing to change or overturn what is established
Term names the service of God as the central obligation of Israel's life — the commanded inner orientation of devoted service to the Lord as opposed to other gods
Term names Levitical priestly service as an enacted inner-being vocation — ministry in the tabernacle/temple as a calling expressing devotion
Term names diligent labour as an expression of inner wisdom — the wise person's orientation toward productive work contrasted with the fool's pursuit of worthless things
Term names the burden of service — worship obligations that weigh the worshiper, or human sin that weighs God, revealing the inner weight of the covenantal relationship
Term names temple/tabernacle ministry as a vocation expressing the community's inner orientation toward God — ordered service requiring wholehearted devotion
Term names work as a human vocation and moral measure — the ordinary labour of life, and the fruit of righteous inner orientation producing inner peace
Term names purposeful action — especially God's acts of wonder and Daniel's act of prayer — naming inner devotion through prayer and divine inner attributes
Term names service as an inner-being experience — both oppressive bondage producing inward groaning, and devoted service originating in a stirred heart and spirit
Term names slavery as an inner-being condition of bondage — experienced as degradation but also as the context for divine faithfulness
Term names human works as objects of divine moral scrutiny — acts whose inner origins are known to God and subject to judgment
Term names human works as resting in God's hand — linking inner character to outward acts under divine oversight
Term (particle of entreaty) discloses the inner posture of urgent, humble pleading before God — the orientation of the person in the moment of prayer or anguished appeal
Term names the act of prayer — the deliberate inner orientation of the person toward God in petition, thanksgiving, and intercession
